metadata.context: PRD review: Ockham Wave 1 Foundation (5 agents: architecture, correctness, safety, quality, performance)

findings.title: `ic state list` has no `--json` flag — F3's bulk pre-fetch assumption is wrong
findings.description: F3 AC assumes `ic state list ockham_offset --json` returns values; actual output is scope_ids only. Bulk pre-fetch pattern cannot be implemented as specified. Fallback is O(N) per-bead `ic state get` calls.
findings.fix: Either extend ic state list with `--json --with-values` flag (intercore dependency), or update AC to specify per-bead pattern and accept O(N) cost.

findings.title: Lane is a label, not a top-level field — bead-to-theme mapping requires per-bead `bd show`, not `bd list --json`
findings.description: PRD brainstorm assumes `bd list --json | jq '.[] | {id, lane}'` but lane is stored as `labels[]` entry containing 'lane:<name>', not a top-level field. This means each bead requires individual `bd show` call.
findings.fix: Update F2 AC to use per-bead pattern: `bd show <bead_id> --json | jq '.labels[]? | select(startswith("lane:"))' | ltrimstr("lane:")`

findings.title: `agent_reliability(agent, domain)` does not exist — interspect exposes calibration stats, not that interface
findings.description: Brainstorm specifies `interspect.agent_reliability(agent, domain) -> {hit_rate, sessions, confidence, last_active}` but this function does not exist. Interspect calibration outputs `confidence.json` (a file), not an API. Domain dimension is absent from evidence schema.
findings.fix: Either read confidence.json directly in Ockham and map schema, or add thin adapter. Mark interspect F6 dependency as 'Requires wiring' not 'Available'.

findings.title: F3 integration requires the write side (F2's `ockham dispatch advise`) before the read side works end-to-end
findings.description: F3 reads offsets from ic state, but these offsets are written by F2. Shipping F3 before F2 means offset wiring has no source; integration cycle breaks.
findings.fix: Sequence features: F2 (write offsets) → F3 (read and apply offsets). Mark as prerequisite.

findings.title: Interspect has no halt-record write path — double-sentinel's 'agent-unwritable' guarantee is unimplemented
findings.description: F7 specifies `ockham check` reconstructs factory-paused.json from interspect halt record if file deleted. But no AC specifies how Ockham writes the halt record to interspect in the first place.
findings.fix: Add AC to F7 specifying halt-record write path to interspect before reconstruction can work. Define event schema: type='ockham-factory-halt', status=active|cleared.

findings.title: `cost-query.sh` has no per-theme/per-lane query mode — drift detection baseline requires new interstat work
findings.description: F5 requires cost and cycle time grouped by theme. Neither interstat nor lib-discovery expose per-theme aggregations. Ockham either needs direct SQLite access or interstat must add new query modes.
findings.fix: Either extend interstat with theme-grouped queries, or accept direct SQLite access from Ockham's internal/scoring package. File separate dependency ticket.

findings.title: `ic lane status` is currently broken with SQL error — CONSTRAIN path in lib-dispatch.sh depends on a command returning an error
findings.description: The CONSTRAIN check in dispatch_rescore calls `ic lane status` which has a SQL error. This is a pre-existing issue but F3 integration may expose it.
findings.fix: Verify ic lane status works; if not, fix before F3 integration.

findings.title: Package name mismatch: AGENTS.md uses `dispatch`, PRD introduces `scoring` and `governor` — stubs exist but are empty
findings.description: PRD names internal packages as `internal/scoring` and `internal/governor`, but existing Ockham stubs use `internal/dispatch`. Creates confusion at implementation start.
findings.fix: Clarify canonical package names. Recommend: keep PRD names (scoring, governor), update/rename stubs.

findings.title: Ratchet AC calls `intercept decide` for logging but intercept's interface takes a gate name and structured JSON, not a free log record
findings.description: F6 says to log ratchet decisions through `intercept decide`, but intercept is a decision gate (returns verdict), not a log sink. Requires defining a named gate contract.
findings.fix: Define ratchet gate contract (e.g., `ratchet-decision` gate). Clarify that distillation of logged decisions is Wave 2 non-goal.

findings.title: low=-3 magnitude is absent from ACs; only high=+6 is specified
findings.description: F1 AC correctly states all three magnitudes (high=+6, normal=0, low=-3), but F2 scoring AC only specifies clamp [-6, +6]. Developer may implement symmetric low=-6 instead of asymmetric low=-3, violating INV-02. Not caught by 80% coverage AC unless test explicitly encodes exact magnitude.
findings.fix: Add to F2 AC: 'Priority-to-offset mapping is: high=+6, normal=0, low=-3. The Scoring package enforces these values (not just the clamp).'

findings.title: Floor-guard position in CONSTRAIN path is ambiguous in F3 — frozen theme score would be re-raised from 0 to 1
findings.description: F3 AC says 'CONSTRAIN check → frozen theme → score=0, skip' but the floor guard (step 4) clamps 0 back to 1. A frozen bead gets dispatched at score=1 instead of being excluded entirely. Existing lib-dispatch.sh correctly uses 'continue' to exclude, but AC phrasing could mislead implementer.
findings.convergence: 3 agents (Correctness primary, Quality-03 references, Safety-006 references)
findings.fix: Rewrite AC: 'If bead's theme is frozen, exclude from candidate set entirely (do not add to result; skip all remaining steps including floor guard).' Remove 'score=0' phrasing.

findings.title: Fresh evidence requirement AC is incomplete — the timer resets on demotion, but the AC does not specify which demotion events reset it
findings.description: AC states 'after demotion, re-promotion requires >= 10 sessions timestamped after demotion event' but does not enumerate which demotions reset the counter. Implementation may only reset on explicit BYPASS (salient) and silently skip CONSTRAIN demotions (frequent), allowing snap-back promotion via stale evidence.
findings.fix: Add AC: 'The fresh evidence counter resets on ALL demotion events, including automatic CONSTRAIN demotions, not only BYPASS demotions. Sessions predating demotion timestamp are excluded from guard evaluation.'

findings.title: `ockham resume` reset atomicity not specified in F7 AC
findings.description: Vision.md specifies resume must reset all domains to supervised atomically, but F7 AC has no atomicity constraint. If process crashes after resetting 3 of 7 domains, factory resumes with mixed autonomy state; some domains skip the required confirmation window.
findings.fix: Add `resume-in-progress` marker. Complete full reset before clearing marker. Add transaction semantics to domain state writes.

findings.title: Cross-domain min-tier AC is present but missing the 'any frozen domain → ineligible' rule
findings.description: F6 AC states 'Cross-domain beads: authority resolves to min(tier_per_domain)' but omits brainstorm rule: 'If any touched domain is frozen (CONSTRAIN), the bead is ineligible for dispatch regardless of other domains' status.' Min-tier alone doesn't capture this.
findings.fix: Add to F6 AC: 'If any domain touched by a bead is frozen (CONSTRAIN), exclude from candidate set entirely, regardless of computed min-tier. Frozen-domain ineligibility takes precedence.'

findings.title: The Tier 3 trigger AC omits the 'at reduced oversight' qualifier definition
findings.description: AC includes 'operating at reduced oversight' but doesn't define it testably. Could mean different things: >=1 domain autonomous, no recent human review, explicit flag. Without definition, qualifier is ignored or implemented inconsistently.
findings.fix: Define: 'Operating at reduced oversight' means at least one domain is supervised or autonomous. If all at shadow, Tier 3 does not trigger regardless of distinct root cause count.'

findings.title: Feedback loop suppression during ratchet demotion has no AC
findings.description: Brainstorm specifies suppress weight_drift signals during ratchet demotion (SYS-NEW-01) to prevent self-reinforcing degradation spiral. No F5 AC enforces this; feature is silently absent from implementation.
findings.fix: Add F5 AC: 'Suppress weight_drift signals for a domain undergoing ratchet demotion, until ratchet stabilizes (one confirmation window after demotion).'

findings.title: Cold-start partial-evidence case not specified
findings.description: Cold-start rules specify evidence meeting autonomous guard still starts at supervised (INV-08), but the AC does not address the edge case where evidence meets some but not all autonomous guard conditions.
findings.fix: Add AC clarifying: 'If cold-start evidence meets any single guard condition (hit_rate, sessions, or confidence) but not all three, start at shadow, not supervised.'

findings.title: 30-day re-confirmation staggering has no enforcement AC
findings.description: Brainstorm specifies staggering by promotion timestamp to prevent cluster expirations, but no AC verifies this staggering is enforced at runtime.
findings.fix: Add AC: 'Re-confirmations are evaluated per domain, using promotion_timestamp of that domain, not a global clock. No two domains re-confirm on the same day unless they were actually promoted on the same day.'

findings.title: Starvation detection absent from ACs and Non-Goals
findings.description: Vision.md specifies starvation prevention (INV-20): theme receiving zero dispatches over configurable cadence → Tier 1 INFORM. Not in F5 ACs and not in Non-Goals, creating ambiguity about whether it's Wave 1 or deferred.
findings.fix: Either add to F5 AC or explicitly list in Non-Goals. Recommend: deferral to Wave 2 with Anomaly subsystem.

findings.title: `ockham health --json` schema for ratchet state is underspecified
findings.description: AC lists JSON fields at category level but doesn't specify ratchet_state schema. Cannot deterministically test output without knowing expected structure.
findings.fix: Add to F7 AC: 'ratchet_state is array of {domain, tier, sessions_since_demotion, reconfirmation_due_at}.'

findings.title: Logging AC for dispatch does not include the three required fields
findings.description: F3 AC specifies logging `raw_score`, `ockham_offset`, `final_score` but existing dispatch log only has `score` field. Schema migration not called out; downstream consumers (calibration, interstat) need backward-compatibility handling.
findings.fix: Add AC: 'Dispatch log schema extended to {ts, session, bead, raw_score, ockham_offset, final_score, outcome}. Existing entries without ockham_offset interpreted as 0.'

findings.title: `ic state list <k>` returns scope_ids only — bulk value fetch assumed by PRD does not exist
findings.description: Same as A-01. Convergence confirms blocking issue.
findings.convergence: 3 agents (Quality confirms A-01, Architecture initial, Performance notes)
findings.fix: Extend ic or revert to per-bead pattern.

findings.title: `agent_reliability()` interface assumed but does not exist in interspect
findings.description: Same as A-03. Convergence confirms blocking issue.
findings.fix: Map from confidence.json or add interspect wiring.

findings.title: CONSTRAIN check in F3 references frozen themes but Tier 2 CONSTRAIN is a Wave 2 non-goal — the AC will fail on day 1
findings.description: F3 AC assumes CONSTRAIN signals are available (frozen themes), but the anomaly subsystem that produces CONSTRAIN is explicitly deferred to Wave 2. The AC references a non-existent signal source.
findings.fix: Either move anomaly/CONSTRAIN detection to Wave 1, or clarify F3's CONSTRAIN path is a Wave 2 stub. If stub, add to non-goals explicitly.

findings.title: Duplicate AC: 30-day re-confirmation appears identically in both F4 and F6 with no ownership resolution
findings.description: Same AC listed under both F4 (SessionStart hook) and F6 (ratchet); unclear which feature owns implementation and testing.
findings.fix: Clarify: F6 owns the re-confirmation timer logic; F4 ensures SessionStart calls it via `ockham check`.

findings.title: Cycle time per theme is not available from any listed dependency
findings.description: Same as A-06. F5 needs cycle time aggregated per theme; no dependency currently provides this.
findings.fix: Extend interstat or accept direct SQLite access.

findings.title: 'Not a quality arbiter' and 'Not an audit log' from brainstorm § 8 are absent from the non-goals section
findings.description: Brainstorm specifies two explicit non-goals; PRD non-goals section omits them, creating scope ambiguity.
findings.fix: Add to non-goals: 'Not a quality arbiter. Not an audit log. Starvation detection deferred to Wave 2 with Anomaly subsystem. Delegation ceiling (INV-2) deferred to Wave 3.'

findings.title: No AC tests the negative: a bead in a frozen theme is NOT selectable despite a positive offset
findings.description: ACs test positive cases (offset applied) but lack negative test: frozen theme with ockham_offset=+6 must NOT be selectable. Tests must verify exclusion, not just non-interference.
findings.fix: Add AC: 'A bead with ockham_offset=+6 in a frozen theme is NOT selectable. Test: score it with CONSTRAIN=true, verify excluded from result, reason logged as CONSTRAIN_FROZEN.'

findings.title: `ockham health --json` AC duplicates pleasure signal output already specified in F5's `ockham health --json` AC
findings.description: Both F5 and F7 define `ockham health --json` output with overlapping fields; unclear which owns the endpoint.
findings.fix: Consolidate to F7 (read-only diagnostics). F5 covers pleasure-signal writing only.

findings.title: F2 names packages `internal/scoring` and `internal/governor` but Ockham directory has `internal/dispatch` (not `scoring`)
findings.description: Same as A-08. Convergence confirms naming mismatch.
findings.fix: Clarify canonical names; update or rename stubs.

findings.title: Baseline prediction ('predicted baseline per theme') has no specified source or bootstrapping procedure for themes with no history
findings.description: Drift detection compares actual vs predicted baseline, but new themes with no history have no baseline. AC doesn't specify cold-start baseline (neutral? median? exponential smoothing?).
findings.fix: Add AC: 'For themes with <10 completed beads, use neutral baseline (no drift signal). After 10 beads, compute rolling baseline as median cycle time.'

findings.title: Dispatch token contract absent — INV-1 enforcement deferred without specification
findings.description: INV-1 requires dispatch token validated against Clavain-minted token, not `$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` (which agents can spoof). Authority package deferred to Wave 3, but token mint spec must exist in Wave 1 or Wave 3 rediscovers OCKHAM-01 spoofing attack.
findings.fix: Add F4 AC: 'Clavain's SessionStart hook writes dispatch token (random 32-byte hex) to ~/.clavain/dispatch-tokens/<session-epoch>.tok, readable only by invoking process. ockham check reads and hashes it; stores hash in signals.db for Wave 3 authority validation.'

findings.title: signals.db corruption has no recovery path and no schema version
findings.description: SessionStart hook is `ockham check 2>/dev/null || true`, fail-open. If signals.db is corrupt, error is silently swallowed. Ratchet timers freeze. Halt reconstruction fails silently. At halt time, factory resumes without sentinel enforcement.
findings.fix: Add PRAGMA user_version=1. Add integrity_check on open. On corrupt: backup file, reinit from interspect authority snapshot. Add ockham check --repair. Add Clavain doctor check for last-check-ok health file.

findings.title: INV-8 enumeration gap — ockham_offset ic state writes not guarded during halt
findings.description: F7 AC guards CLI entry points (`ockham intent`, `ockham check`) but not the package layer. `internal/governor.Evaluate()` writes to ic state directly. A caller using the Go package directly bypasses CLI halt guard and publishes offsets while factory halted.
findings.fix: Guard must be at `internal/governor.Evaluate()` entry point. Read halt sentinel (both factory-paused.json and signals.db flag); return ErrFactoryHalted if active. CLI guard is secondary check.

findings.title: Double-sentinel reconstruction path does not specify the interspect query contract
findings.description: F7 AC says 'ockham check reconstructs factory-paused.json from interspect halt record if file deleted.' No AC specifies which interspect table, event type, or fields identify a halt record. Implementer may read signals.db (agent-writable) instead of interspect.
findings.fix: Add AC: 'Query interspect for event type ockham-factory-halt with status=active. Resume events set status=cleared. Reconstruction only on records where status=active AND session_epoch matches current factory instance.'

findings.title: Pleasure signal values in signals.db are used for ratchet promotion — no freshness check
findings.description: Pleasure signals written to signals.db for ratchet consumption, but no AC specifies how old a value can be before treated as stale. If principal offline for 72 hours, signals are 72 hours stale at re-confirmation time; ratchet may promote based on stale data.
findings.fix: Add AC: 'Pleasure signals have computed_at timestamp. Ratchet rejects signals >48h old for promotion decisions; logs signal-staleness-warn. For re-confirmation, if most recent signal >48h old, postpone re-confirmation until fresh evaluation.'

findings.title: Evaluation order specified but no AC with a pass/fail test for order violations
findings.description: INV-03 specifies order: CONSTRAIN → offset → perturbation → floor. Prose is correct but no testable AC verifies order is enforced. Freeze bypass and floor-guard inversion undetected.
findings.fix: Add falsifiable AC: 'A frozen-theme bead with ockham_offset=+6 logs raw_score=0, final_score=0 (floor guard does not apply). Any test showing final_score>0 for frozen theme fails.'

findings.title: Cold-start conservative cap (no direct autonomous assignment) has no AC verifying it is enforced
findings.description: INV-08 specifies evidence meeting autonomous guard still starts at supervised, not autonomous. No F6 AC verifies this cap is enforced; implementer may assign autonomous directly if evidence qualifies.
findings.fix: Add AC: 'Cold-start logic: if evidence meets all autonomous guard conditions AND is first evidence for domain, assign supervised tier, not autonomous. Test: provide qualifying evidence, verify tier=supervised on cold start.'

findings.title: Cross-domain failure attribution not specified — data model gap before Wave 3 authority package
findings.description: Multi-domain beads touch multiple authority domains, but evidence schema has no domain column. Ratchet evidence written to wrong domain. Known gap before Wave 3.
findings.fix: Add to non-goals: 'Cross-domain failure attribution (Wave 2/3) — evidence schema augmentation required.'

findings.title: `ockham resume --constrained` permitted write scope unspecified — unfreeze during halt may cause dispatch burst
findings.description: F7 allows `ockham resume --constrained` to permit intent changes while factory paused, but what other writes are allowed is unspecified. Unfreeze + immediate dispatch burst is possible.
findings.fix: Enumerate permitted writes under --constrained: intent changes yes, ratchet no, offset no. Add AC: 'ockham resume --constrained permits only ockham intent writes; all ratchet and offset writes remain blocked.'

findings.title: `ockham check` on SessionStart blocks 200-500ms with no rate limit guard
findings.description: PRD specifies `ockham check 2>/dev/null || true` in SessionStart with no TTL sentinel. Without guard, runs on every session (including 5-10 compactions/day). At 50 open beads + interstat queries, 200-500ms per run. Existing `bd doctor` pattern should apply.
findings.fix: Add 5-minute TTL sentinel to SessionStart wiring. Copy existing `bd doctor` pattern: check `/tmp/clavain-ockham-check-${USER}` mtime, only run if >300 seconds old. `--dry-run` bypasses sentinel.

findings.title: Per-bead `ic state get` in dispatch_rescore replaced by bulk pre-fetch, but the pre-fetch output format needs definition
findings.description: Same as A-01 / Q-1. F3 assumes ic state list returns values; must verify before implementing.
findings.fix: Verify: run `ic state list ockham_offset --json` after writing test offset, confirm output includes values not just scope_ids.

findings.title: Per-bead `bd dep list` and `bd show` in dispatch_rescore are the current dominant cost, not ockham
findings.description: Current dispatch_rescore already spends 6+ seconds on per-bead bd calls at 91 beads. Ockham's 4ms offset lookup is negligible but F3 should not add per-bead work (e.g., `ockham dispatch advise --json` per bead). Separate issue but relevant to F3 implementation.
findings.fix: File separate bead for batch bd API. F3 implementation must use in-memory offset map from bulk pre-fetch, not subprocess per bead.

findings.title: 7-day rolling window for drift detection uses full table scan at first query
findings.description: Drift threshold query requires 7-day window over agent_runs table. At 18k rows, scan is 50ms with timestamp index, acceptable. Risk: if query construction changes (per-theme queries, different WHERE conditions), planner may choose wrong index.
findings.fix: Issue single query returning all beads in window, group by bead_id in Go. Do not issue per-theme SQL queries. Timestamp index will keep scan bounded at ~5-10% of table.

findings.title: `ockham check` subprocess startup cost dominates at current data volumes
findings.description: If `ockham check` shells out to `cost-query.sh` for pleasure signals, subprocess overhead (30-50ms per invocation, 90-150ms for 3 signals) dominates actual query time (1-20ms). Correct design: Go binary reads interstat metrics.db directly via sqlite3 driver.
findings.fix: internal/scoring should open ~/.claude/interstat/metrics.db directly in Go. cost-query.sh is external interface; not needed inside binary.

findings.title: Per-bead offset model is feasible at 100 beads; watch at 1,000
findings.description: Per-bead ockham_offset in intercore state is O(1) lookup at 100 beads, acceptable. At 1,000 beads (12-18 months away), still <10ms. Real concern: closed beads accumulate stale entries. At 600+ closed beads, list returns mixed stale+live entries, forcing filter in dispatch.
findings.fix: Add `ic state delete ockham_offset <bead_id>` to bead-close path, or use 7-day TTL on ic state set calls.

improvements: All 5 agents confirm needs-changes verdict independently
Interface issues (ic state list, agent_reliability) discovered by multiple agents; cross-validation confirms blocking
Correctness bugs (floor guard, atomicity, fresh evidence) are clear and have concrete failure paths
Safety gaps are systematic: signals.db corruption, dispatch token contract, halt guard layer
Performance gaps are actionable: TTL sentinel is 6 lines, query verification is one-time check

discourse_health.notes: All 5 agents engaged robustly; 87% of findings are unique (not duplicated). Two findings converge at 3+ agents (ic state list, lane field) indicating convergent discovery. Relevance high: all findings directly reference PRD sections and ACs. Participation even: each agent covers 9-12 findings (gini 0.35 is healthy). Flow degraded due to high P1 count (15 blocking issues) indicating pre-implementation review caught systemic gaps rather than polish issues.

synthesis_notes: Review validates PRD review process. Five independent agents discovered 47 total findings, converging on three critical interface gaps (ic state list, agent_reliability, lane field) that would block implementation. Distinctly specialized findings (Safety-002 signals.db corruption, Perf-001 TTL sentinel, Correct-02 floor guard) indicate agents applied domain expertise. All P1 findings have concrete failure paths and minimal remediation (add AC, verify interface, or 6-line code change). Wave 1 is implementable but requires P1 fixes before coding starts.
